title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
summary_zh: 术前准备：建立静脉通路、给予镇静药物、全身麻醉、插入气管导管辅助呼吸、留置导尿管；手术一般耗时3至6小时
text_en: Before the surgery, an intravenous line will be started and you may be given a medication to help you relax. Cabbage procedures are done under general anesthesia, which will put you to sleep for the duration of the operation. A breathing tube will be inserted through your mouth and into your throat to help you breathe. A catheter will be placed in your bladder to drain your urine. Coronary artery bypass surgery generally takes 3 to 6 hours.

title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
summary_zh: 常规搭桥手术开胸过程：沿胸骨正中切开皮肤和胸骨、撑开肋骨暴露心脏，连接体外循环（心肺机）以在手术期间暂时替代心肺功能，必要时停跳心脏进行吻合
text_en: During a conventional bypass surgery, your surgeon begins by making an incision in the skin over your breastbone or sternum. He or she will then cut the sternum and move your ribcage in order to get to your heart. Throughout the procedure, your circulatory system may be connected to a cardiopulmonary bypass pump or heart lung machine. This machine may temporarily perform the functions of your heart and lungs during the surgery, allowing your heart to be stopped if desired while the surgeon shows the grafts into place.

title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
summary_zh: 内乳动脉（胸廓内动脉）搭桥：常用移植血管包括胸廓内动脉和大隐静脉；内乳动脉搭桥时保留其上端与锁骨下动脉的连接，将下端从胸壁游离后吻合至冠状动脉阻塞远端
text_en: Different blood vessels may typically be used for the grafts. The internal thoracic arteries in the chest, also known as internal mammary arteries, or the saffinous veins in the legs. For the internal thoracic artery graft, your surgeon will leave the upper end attached to the subclavian artery and divert the lower end from your chest wall to your coronary artery just beyond the blockage. Your surgeon will then sew the graft into place.

title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
summary_zh: 大隐静脉搭桥：将静脉一端缝合至主动脉、另一端缝合至阻塞远端的冠状动脉；吻合完成后通过电信号恢复心跳并安装临时起搏器
text_en: For the saffinous vein graft, your surgeon will suture one end to the aorta and the other end to the narrowed artery just beyond the blockage. With the grafts securely in place, your surgeon may use electrical signals to restore the heart beat and attach a temporary pacemaker to the heart.

title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
summary_zh: 关胸与术后引流：确认心跳恢复正常后断开心肺机，用钢丝固定胸骨、缝合皮肤切口，放置临时胸腔引流管
text_en: Once your heart is again beating normally, if the surgeon used the heart lung machine, it would be disconnected. Your surgeon will wire the breastbone back together and suture the skin incision closed. A temporary drainage tube will be placed through the skin beneath the incision.

title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
summary_zh: 非体外循环（Off-Pump）搭桥与微创手术：不使用心肺机、心脏持续跳动，用机械稳定器固定吻合区域；微创术式通过小切口或端口使用特殊器械完成
text_en: When the surgeon chooses to do the surgery without using the heart lung machine, the heart continues to beat. This may be referred to as off-pump bypass surgery or minimally invasive surgery. Instead, a mechanical device is used to steady the part of the heart where the grafting is being done. Surgeons perform minimally invasive bypass procedures using specially designed instruments inserted through small incisions or ports in the chest.

title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
summary_zh: 术后ICU监护与恢复：持续心电监护、必要时使用临时起搏导线控制心率、保留胸腔引流管和导尿管、拔除气管导管改用氧气面罩；术后3至5天逐步撤除各类管路
text_en: After surgery, you will be taken to the intensive care unit. The activity of your heart will be carefully monitored. If necessary, pacing wires will be used to temporarily control your heart rate. The chest tube will remain in place to drain excess blood and air from the chest cavity. Once you can breathe on your own, your breathing tube will be removed and replaced with an oxygen mask. The bladder catheter will remain in place. As you recover from surgery over the next three to five days, all of these devices will be gradually removed.

file: ANCE00199S101_What is a Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)_.mp4
title: ANCE00199S101 What is a Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
summary_zh: 冠状动脉搭桥术（CABG）的定义与目的：通过手术改善心肌的血液循环
text_en: A coronary artery bypass graft, or cabbage, is performed to improve circulation to the heart muscle.

file: ANCE00199S101_What is a Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)_.mp4
title: ANCE00199S101 What is a Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
summary_zh: 搭桥手术的具体过程：取身体其他部位的健康动脉或静脉，连接到阻塞的冠状动脉上，绕过阻塞部分，将富氧血液输送至心肌；一次手术可同时对多条冠状动脉进行搭桥
text_en: In this procedure, a healthy artery or vein from another part of the body is connected or grafted to the blocked coronary artery. The grafted artery or vein bypasses the blocked portion of the coronary artery, carrying oxygen-rich blood to the heart muscle. One or more coronary arteries may be bypassed during a single operation.

file: ANCE00199S102_What Blood Vessels are Commonly Used as a Graft for a Heart Bypass_.mp4
title: ANCE00199S102 What Blood Vessels are Commonly Used as a Graft for a Heart Bypass
summary_zh: 介绍心脏搭桥手术常用的血管移植物类型：胸廓内动脉（又称乳内动脉）和腿部大隐静脉
text_en: Different blood vessels may typically be used for the grafts. The internal thoracic arteries in the chest, also known as internal mammary arteries, or the saffonous veins in the legs.

file: ANCE00199S102_What Blood Vessels are Commonly Used as a Graft for a Heart Bypass_.mp4
title: ANCE00199S102 What Blood Vessels are Commonly Used as a Graft for a Heart Bypass
summary_zh: 胸廓内动脉移植物的手术方法：上端保留与锁骨下动脉的连接，下端从胸壁分离后缝合至冠状动脉阻塞远端
text_en: For the internal thoracic artery graft, your surgeon will leave the upper end attached to the subclavian artery, and divert the lower end from your chest wall to your coronary artery, just beyond the blockage. Your surgeon will then sew the graft into place.

file: ANCE00199S102_What Blood Vessels are Commonly Used as a Graft for a Heart Bypass_.mp4
title: ANCE00199S102 What Blood Vessels are Commonly Used as a Graft for a Heart Bypass
summary_zh: 大隐静脉移植物的手术方法：一端缝合至主动脉，另一端缝合至狭窄冠状动脉的阻塞远端
text_en: For the saffonous vein graft, your surgeon will suture one end to the aorta, and the other end to the narrowed artery, just beyond the blockage.

file: ANCE00199S103_What are the Different Types of Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedures_.mp4
title: ANCE00199S103 What are the Different Types of Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedures
summary_zh: 常规冠状动脉搭桥手术（体外循环下）：外科医生沿胸骨做切口，切开胸骨并撑开肋骨暴露心脏，术中将循环系统连接至体外循环机（心肺机）以维持血液循环
text_en: During a conventional bypass surgery, your surgeon begins by making an incision in the skin over your breastbone or sternum. He or she will then cut the sternum and move your ribcage in order to get to your heart. Throughout the procedure, your circulatory system may be connected to a cardiopulmonary bypass pump or heart lung machine.

file: ANCE00199S103_What are the Different Types of Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedures_.mp4
title: ANCE00199S103 What are the Different Types of Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedures
summary_zh: 非体外循环搭桥手术（off-pump CABG）：不使用心肺机，心脏在术中持续跳动，也称为不停跳搭桥或微创手术；使用机械稳定装置固定心脏上需要进行吻合的区域，以便精确操作
text_en: When the surgeon chooses to do the surgery without using the heart lung machine, the heart continues to beat. This may be referred to as off-pump bypass surgery or minimally invasive surgery. Instead, a mechanical device is used to steady the part of the heart where the grafting is being done.

file: ANCE00199S103_What are the Different Types of Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedures_.mp4
title: ANCE00199S103 What are the Different Types of Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedures
summary_zh: 微创搭桥手术：外科医生通过胸壁上的小切口或端口，使用专门设计的器械完成搭桥操作，避免了传统的大切口开胸
text_en: Surgeons perform minimally invasive bypass procedures using specially designed instruments inserted through small incisions or ports in the chest.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
summary_zh: 外科引流管的基本介绍：引流管的作用是排出手术部位皮下积液，预防感染并促进愈合；引流装置由引流管和收集球囊组成
text_en: Curgical drains remove fluid from under your skin near your surgical site. This helps prevent infection and encourages healing. A surgical drain consists of a drain tube that runs from your surgical site to a bulb that collects the fluid.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
summary_zh: 引流管护理概述与准备工作：需要定期挤捋引流管以清除血凝块，球囊半满时排空；所需物品包括酒精棉片、量杯和引流记录表，必要时佩戴一次性手套
text_en: You strip the drain tube to keep it clear and remove any clots or blockages. Then you empty the bulb when it is half full or as instructed by your healthcare provider. You will need alcohol wipes, a measuring cup and a drainage record sheet. Be sure to wear disposable gloves if your healthcare provider says you should.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
summary_zh: 操作前注意事项与前置步骤（步骤1-2）：避免拉扯引流管，用肥皂水洗手并擦干，按医嘱佩戴一次性手套
text_en: Be careful not to pull on the tubing. You should not feel any tugging where the tube enters your skin. Step 1. Wash your hands with soap and water and then dry them. Step 2. Put on disposable gloves if your healthcare provider says you should.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
summary_zh: 检查与松解血凝块（步骤3-5）：检查引流管内是否有阻塞血凝块（包括皮肤下隐藏的），轻轻挤压管壁松解凝块，一只手在皮肤出口处固定引流管
text_en: Step 3. Look for clots or blockages that may prevent the fluid from flowing out of the tube and into the bulb. Some clots may be hidden inside the tube under your skin. Step 4. Loosen the clots by gently squeezing the tubes surrounding them. Step 5. Use one hand to hold the drain tube in place where it leaves your skin.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
summary_zh: 挤捋引流管操作（步骤6-7及重复）：用酒精棉片夹住引流管，沿管滑动手指将液体挤入球囊；可能需要重复多次，中途不要松手，若松手需换新酒精棉片重新操作
text_en: Step 6. Use your other hand to pinch the tube with an alcohol wipe between your finger and thumb. Step 7. Slide your pinched fingers along the tube to force any fluid out of the tube and into the bulb. You may need to repeat steps 5 through 7 several times to clear the tube. Try not to let go of the tube between steps. If fluid remains in the tube or you accidentally let go, repeat steps 5 through 7 using a new alcohol wipe.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
summary_zh: 排空球囊准备（步骤8-10）：球囊不能超过半满，否则影响吸引力；将球囊置于切口下方使液体流入球囊，球囊远离身体，取下瓶盖前不可挤压球囊
text_en: Do not allow the bulb to become more than half full. Too much fluid in the bulb reduces its ability to remove fluid from underneath your skin. Now you will begin emptying the bulb. Step 8. Hold the bulb lower than your incision so that fluid moves out of the tube and into the bulb. Step 9. Point the bulb away from your body. Never squeeze the bulb before taking the cap off. Step 10. Remove the cap.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
summary_zh: 排空球囊并重建负压（步骤11-14）：裸手不可触碰开口，将球囊倒置挤出液体至量杯；排空后持续挤压球囊并用酒精棉片擦拭顶部，保持挤压状态盖回瓶盖，使球囊形成持续负压吸引
text_en: Never touch the opening with your bare hands. Step 11. Hold the measuring cup under the bulb. Step 12. Turn the bulb upside down and squeeze the fluid into the cup. Step 13. After removing the fluid, continue squeezing the bulb and use a new alcohol wipe to clean the top. Step 14. While still squeezing the bulb, put the cap back on the top. The depressed bulb creates suction that continuously removes fluid from underneath your skin.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
summary_zh: 记录与日常维护（步骤15-17）：读取量杯中液体量并记录在记录表上，按指示清洗量杯；日常保持球囊低于切口水平以利引流
text_en: Step 15. Read the amount of fluid in the measuring cup. Step 16. Write the amount on your record sheet. Step 17. Empty and rinse the cup as directed. Keep the bulb below the level of your incision to help the fluid move out of the tube and into the bulb.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
summary_zh: 警示信号与就医指征：引流液量突然增减、气味改变、出现脓液或变稠、引流管脱出、切口裂开、切口红肿疼痛化脓、体温达101°F以上时应联系外科医生
text_en: Contact your surgeon if you notice the amount of fluid suddenly increases or decreases, the odor of the fluid changes. The fluid contains pus or becomes thicker over time. Your drain tube falls out or your incision opens. Your incision is red, swollen, painful or has pus coming out or your temperature is 101 degrees Fahrenheit or higher.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
summary_zh: 步骤1——目视检查：观察术侧足部和脚趾，与另一侧对比；正常应温暖、肤色正常、无明显肿胀，这些是血流良好的表现
text_en: Step 1. Look at your foot and toes on the leg recovering from the surgical procedure. Compare your feet. Do you see any differences? Your foot and toes on the surgical side should feel warm, be normal in color, and have little to no swelling. These signs mean you have good blood flow in your leg.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
summary_zh: 血流不良的警示信号：足部发红或发紫、触感凉冷、严重肿胀，提示可能存在血液循环障碍
text_en: If your foot looks red or blue, feels cool, or has a lot of swelling, your leg may have poor blood flow.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
summary_zh: 步骤2——感觉检查：足部麻木或刺痛可能是血流不良的征兆，但也可能是神经阻滞麻醉尚未消退所致，不确定时应咨询医生
text_en: Step 2. Does your foot feel numb or tingly? A feeling of numbness and tingling may also be a sign of poor blood flow. Or, you may feel numbness from a type of anesthesia called a nerve block until it wears off. If you are not sure if you had a nerve block, ask your surgeon.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
summary_zh: 步骤3——活动脚趾：扭动脚趾有助于促进足部血液循环；以上三步应每4小时重复一次或遵医嘱
text_en: Step 3. Wiggle your toes. Wiggling your toes may improve blood flow to your foot. Repeat steps 1 through 3 every 4 hours, or as directed by your surgeon.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
summary_zh: 毛细血管再充盈测试介绍：毛细血管是将富氧血液输送到脚趾的微小血管，该测试可评估术侧脚趾的血液循环状况
text_en: Another way to check for good blood flow is by doing a capillary refill test. Capillaries are small blood vessels that carry oxygen-rich blood to your toes. The capillary refill test allows you to see how well blood is flowing through these blood vessels to your toes on the surgical side.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
summary_zh: 毛细血管再充盈测试操作步骤：捏住脚趾趾甲处使其变白后松开，趾甲应在2-3秒内恢复粉色，表明血流良好；每4小时重复一次或遵医嘱
text_en: Step 1. Gently pinch the tip of your toe directly over the toenail with your hand. Step 2. Squeeze until your toenail turns white. Then let go. Your toenail should turn pink again within 2-3 seconds. This sign means that you have good blood flow in your leg and foot. Repeat steps 1 and 2 every 4 hours, or as directed by your surgeon.
